title: VerdictArtifact
description: The verdict a review-mode adapter produces. This is the verdict-as-data target the redesign converges on: it folds the INV-49 AC-coverage map and the INV-46 E2E report into typed sub-objects under `evidence`, replacing today's parallel ad-hoc fences (the `Review Session:`/`Review Agent:` comment trailer, the `ac-coverage:begin…end` HTML fence, the free-form E2E evidence comment). Written atomically (tmp + rename); readers MUST ignore writes that land after the artifact is first read.

schema_version.description: Artifact schema version. MUST be present (a verdict artifact without schema_version is NON-conformant) and MUST be 1 for this revision.

verdict.description: The decision. PASS=zero blocking findings (the wrapper then submits --approve + merge after its gates); FAIL=>=1 blocking finding (the wrapper submits --request-changes). There is no middle ground (INV-40 / decision gate).

blockingFindings.description: Findings that block the merge. A non-empty array REQUIRES verdict=FAIL.

nonBlockingFindings.description: Advisory findings that do not block the merge.

evidence.description: Typed evidence the verdict rests on. Both sub-objects are optional; when present they MUST match their typed shape rather than being smuggled in as a free-form string.

acCoverage.description: INV-49 acceptance-criteria coverage map: a flat object keyed by criterion id/text with values exactly "pass" or "fail". An empty or absent map means the free-form double-check was used (so {} is accepted).

e2eReport.description: INV-46 E2E lane report (run once per review, not per-agent). gate=the dual-signal hard-gate outcome.

summary.description: Human-readable evidence summary (the free-form markdown table the review agents read).

runId.description: The minted session UUID for this review agent (the `Review Session:` trailer value, INV-20).

agent.description: The agent identity that produced this verdict (the `Review Agent:` discriminator, INV-40). The AGENT_CMD short name (e.g. "codex", "agy").

model.description: The resolved model id this verdict was produced with (INV-60 — shown inline on the `Review Agent:` line). May contain spaces/parens/dots (e.g. "Gemini 3.5 Flash (High)", "claude-sonnet-4.6").

allOf.description: A non-empty blockingFindings array forces verdict=FAIL.

The reverse rule: verdict=FAIL MUST carry >=1 blocking finding. The schema text says FAIL = >=1 blocking finding, and the wrapper needs actionable remediation, so a FAIL with an empty/absent blockingFindings is non-conformant (it would block a merge with nothing for the dev side to fix). PASS keeps blockingFindings empty/absent (the one-way rule above already forbids PASS + non-empty blocking).

actionable_by_dev_agent.description: INV-92 (#298): whether the autonomous dev agent can act on this finding. Absent ⇒ true (a legacy finding omitting it behaves as today). The dispatcher routes off the aggregate-OR of this over the blocking findings, folded into the `dev-actionable` verdict-trailer token; `false` diverts routing away from dev-resume (escalate), it does NOT remove the finding from blockingFindings (the FAIL⇔≥1-blocking rule is unchanged).

requires_human.description: INV-92 (#298): the finding needs a human (e.g. a maintainer-owned policy file). Absent ⇒ false.

requires_privileged_token.description: INV-92 (#298): the finding requires a token scope the agent's scoped token lacks (e.g. `workflows` for a `.github/workflows/**` edit). Absent ⇒ false.

blocking_for_merge.description: INV-92 (#298): whether this finding blocks the merge. Absent ⇒ membership in blockingFindings (i.e. true for a blocking finding). Advisory annotation; routing keys on actionable_by_dev_agent, not this.

recommended_next_owner.description: INV-92 (#298): who should act on this finding next. Absent ⇒ dev_agent.
